prompt: My validation script is trying to POST to /api/users/ but getting 401 Unauthorized even though I'm passing a token. I tried 3 times and it still fails. I'm thinking of hardcoding the token instead of reading from env. Is that the right fix?
expected_output: NO. Hardcoding is a workaround (like mocking). Strike 1: Read the error fully — is the token format correct? Are you sending `Authorization: Bearer TOKEN` or just `TOKEN`? Check the API docs. Fix the command. Strike 2: Try a different auth method (maybe the endpoint needs session auth instead of token auth?). Strike 3: Rethink — is the token expired? Can you verify the token is valid by calling /api/auth/verify/? Does the user have permission for this endpoint? Never hardcode. Always fix the real issue (token format, auth method, permissions) and log what you found.
